{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53219","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53219/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53219/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53219/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53219","id":2121685757,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5mMSG6","number":53219,"title":"Refactor CodeInfo/CodeInstance separation and interfaces","user":{"login":"Keno","id":1291671,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyOTE2NzE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1291671?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno","html_url":"https://github.com/Keno","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":11,"created_at":"2024-02-06T21:20:40Z","updated_at":"2024-02-19T08:14:14Z","closed_at":"2024-02-19T01:35:56Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53219","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53219","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53219.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53219.patch","merged_at":"2024-02-19T01:35:56Z"},"body":"The `CodeInfo` type is one of the oldest types in the system and has grown a bit of cruft. In particular, the `rettype`, `inferred`, `parent`, `edges`, `min_world`, `max_world` fields are not used for the original purpose of representing code, but for one or more of (in decreasing order of badness):

1. Smuggling extra results from inference into the compiler
2. Sumggling extra arguments into OpaqueClosure constructors
3. Passing extra information from generated functions to inference

The first of these points in particular causes a fair bit of mixup between caching concerns and compiler concerns and results in external abstract interpreters maintainging their own dummy CodeInfos, just to comply with the interface. Originally, I just wanted to clean up that bit, but it didn't really make sense as a standalone piece, so this PR is more comprehensive.

In particular, this PR:

1. Removes the `parent`, `inferred` and `rettype` fields of `CodeInfo`. They are largely vestigal and code accessing these is probably doing the wrong thing. They should instead be looking at either the CodeInstance or remembering the query that was asked of the cache in the first place.

2. Makes `edges`, `min_world` and `max_world` used for generated functions only. All other uses were replaced by appropriate queries on the CodeInstance. In particular, inference no longer sets these. In the future we may want to consider removing these also and having generated functions return some other object, but that is a topic to revisit once the broader compiler plugins landscape is more clear.

3. Makes the external type inference interface return `CodeInstance` rather than `CodeInfo`. This results in a lot of cleanup, because many functions had multiple code paths, some for CodeInstance and others for fallback to inference/CodeInfo. This is all cleaned up now. If you don't have a CodeInstance, you can ask inference for one. This CodeInstance may or may not be in the cache, but you can look at its types, compile it, etc.

4. Moves the main inference entrypoint out of the codegen library. There is still a little bit of entangelement, but this makes codegen much more of an independent system that you give a CodeInstance and it just fills in the invoke pointer for.

With these changes, only the third use of the above mentioned fields remains.

The overall theme here is decoupling. Over time, various parties have wanted to use the julia compiler with custom IR datastructure, backend code generators, caches, etc. This doesn't quite get us all the way there, but makes inference and codegen much more independent with a clear IR-format-independent interface (CodeInstance).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53219/reactions","total_count":7,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":5,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53219/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1951912465","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53219#issuecomment-1951912465","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53219","id":1951912465,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM50V9IR","user":{"login":"mkitti","id":8062771,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgwNjI3NzE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8062771?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti","html_url":"https://github.com/mkitti","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-19T08:14:13Z","updated_at":"2024-02-19T08:14:13Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"This broke Cthulhu.jl since it accesses parent:
https://github.com/JuliaDebug/Cthulhu.jl/blob/864002c4b573918a60f218e1ef86bc9a8ee3cc0f/TypedSyntax/src/node.jl#L401C1-L402C1","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1951912465/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

dependencies","color":"A87582","default":false,"description":"Involves LLVM, OpenBLAS, or other linked libraries"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":36,"created_at":"2023-02-26T12:58:50Z","updated_at":"2024-02-19T09:52:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"_There has already been an issue that proposed migration to BoringSSL (https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45856), which is not what I propose here._

- Julia already depends on OpenSSL
- Julia currently depends on mbedTLS for:
  - libssh2, on all targets
  - libgit2, on Linux/FreeBSD
  - curl, on Linux/FreeBSD
- All three dependencies could migrate to OpenSSL
- We would cut down the dependency tree
- Avoid relying on an old branch of mbedTLS

From a security perspective, this seems great (fewer updates to manage, and track record shows mbedTLS is frequently behind in Julia).

**Are there any downsides to migrating to OpenSSL and removing mbedTLS?**

If not, and the idea has support, I volunteer to handle the migration PRs in Yggdrasil and julia.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48799/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48799/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1952081619","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48799#issuecomment-1952081619","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48799","id":1952081619,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM50WmbT","user":{"login":"mkitti","id":8062771,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgwNjI3NzE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8062771?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti","html_url":"https://github.com/mkitti","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-19T09:52:53Z","updated_at":"2024-02-19T09:52:53Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"The note [in BRANCHES.md](https://github.com/Mbed-TLS/mbedtls/blob/development/BRANCHES.md) says

The triggering code only seems to cause a problem during precompilation.

```julia
using PrecompileTools
@setup_workload begin
    input = Base.link_pipe!(Pipe(), reader_supports_async=true, writer_supports_async=true)
    term = REPL.Terminals.TTYTerminal("dumb", input.out, devnull, devnull)
    write(input.in, 'q')
    @compile_workload begin
        descend(gcd, (Int, Int); terminal=term)
        # declare we are done with streams
        close(input.in)
    end
end
```

Executing this on release-1.11 does not result in an error.

```julia
using Cthulhu, REPL

function cthulhu_541()
    input = Base.link_pipe!(Pipe(), reader_supports_async=true, writer_supports_async=true)
    term = REPL.Terminals.TTYTerminal("dumb", input.out, devnull, devnull)
    write(input.in, 'q')
    @compile_workload begin
        descend(gcd, (Int, Int); terminal=term)
        # declare we are done with streams
        close(input.in)
end

cthulhu_541()
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1953130578/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
